name: Alhaurín el Grande, Coín, and Guaro Loop
name_original: Ruta por pueblos Coín, Guaro y Monda  – circular desde Alhaurín el Grande
description: This moderate 23.3-mile road cycling route from Alhaurín el Grande takes you through the scenic towns of Coín, Guaro, and Monda.
region_intro: Road cycling around Alhaurín El Grande offers diverse landscapes, from the challenging climbs of the Sierra de Mijas to the gentler terrain of the Guadalhorce Valley. The region is characterized by mountainous areas providing significant elevation changes and valleys with rolling olive groves and citrus orchards. This varied topography allows for a range of road cycling experiences, catering to different preferences for intensity and scenery. The Mediterranean climate supports year-round outdoor activities, making it a consistent destination for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Alhaurín El Grande The most popular road cycling route is Alhaurín el Grande, Coín, and Guaro Loop, a 23.3 miles (37.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 0 minutes to complete. This moderate route connects several local towns through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Mijas – View of Fuengirola loop from Alhaurín el Grande, a moderate 34.4 miles (55.3 km) path. This route offers ascents towards Mijas with views overlooking Fuengirola and the coast. Local road cyclists also love the Café Bar Tejeringos loop from Alhaurín el Grande, a 29.1 miles (46.8 km) trail leading through the local countryside, often completed in about 2 hours 9 minutes. Road cycling around Alhaurín El Grande is defined by the demanding ascents of the Sierra de Mijas, the serene paths through the Guadalhorce Valley, and extensive olive and citrus groves. The network offers options for both challenging climbs and more relaxed rides. The routes in Alhaurín El Grande are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.7 stars from over 24 reviews. More than 1500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Alhaurín El Grande's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Alhaurín El Grande?
A: Alhaurín El Grande offers a diverse landscape for road cycling. You'll find challenging climbs in the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Mijas&lt;/strong&gt; mountains, providing significant elevation changes and sweeping vistas. In contrast, the &lt;strong&gt;Guadalhorce Valley&lt;/strong&gt; offers gentler terrain with picturesque rolling olive groves, citrus orchards, and almond blossom fields, ideal for less strenuous rides.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Alhaurín El Grande?
A: There are over 15 road cycling routes around Alhaurín El Grande, catering to various skill levels. These include easy, moderate, and difficult options, ensuring there's a ride for every cyclist.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Alhaurín El Grande?
A: Thanks to its Mediterranean climate, Alhaurín El Grande is suitable for road cycling year-round. The consistent pleasant weather allows for outdoor activities throughout the seasons, making it a reliable destination for cyclists.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Alhaurín El Grande offers several easier road cycling routes. While specific family-friendly routes are not detailed, there are 4 routes classified as 'easy' that could be suitable for those looking for a less demanding ride.

Q: What are some popular moderate road cycling routes in the area?
A: Many cyclists enjoy the moderate routes here. One popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e932968617/alhaurin-el-grande-coin-and-guaro-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alhaurín el Grande, Coín, and Guaro Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which connects several local towns. Another favorite is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19390090" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mijas – View of Fuengirola loop from Alhaurín el Grande&lt;/a&gt;, offering ascents towards Mijas with coastal views.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, the region offers routes with significant climbs, particularly in the Sierra de Mijas. There are 2 routes classified as 'difficult' that will test your endurance and climbing skills.

Q: What kind of natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: The routes often pass through scenic areas. You might encounter the lush olive and citrus groves of the Guadalhorce Valley. For natural landmarks, you could explore areas near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4498964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco Blanco Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or enjoy views from various peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4019360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mendoza Peak&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2697276" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palomas Peak&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists think about the routes in Alhaurín El Grande?
A: The road cycling routes around Alhaurín El Grande are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 3.7 stars from over 24 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from demanding mountain ascents to serene valley rides, and the beautiful natural scenery.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the routes in Alhaurín El Grande are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38233254" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Café Bar Tejeringos loop from Alhaurín el Grande&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39565741" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path through olive trees – Coín loop from Alhaurín el Grande&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through olive groves or other agricultural areas?
A: Absolutely. The Guadalhorce Valley, which many routes traverse, is characterized by extensive olive groves, citrus orchards, and almond blossom fields. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39565741" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path through olive trees – Coín loop from Alhaurín el Grande&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example of a route that immerses you in these picturesque agricultural landscapes.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the coast?
A: Yes, some routes provide stunning coastal views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19390090" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mijas – View of Fuengirola loop from Alhaurín el Grande&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, includes ascents towards Mijas that offer panoramic vistas overlooking Fuengirola and the Mediterranean coast.
tips: Coín is located in the Guadalhorce Valley, a county and natural region formed by the Guadalhorce River. In May, the end of the orange harvest is celebrated with the Orange Festival.
Walk through the intricate streets of the center, following the banners and crocheted umbrellas.
Some historians say that the battle of Munda between Julius Caesar and the Pompey brothers took place here.

faqs.question: Where does the route start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular loop starting and ending in Alhaurín el Grande. You should be able to find parking within the town to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: As this is a road cycling route, it is generally not suitable for dogs. The focus is on paved roads and cycling, which isn't a safe environment for pets to accompany you.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Andalusia is known for its pleasant climate. Spring and autumn are typically ideal, offering comfortable temperatures for cycling. Summers can be very hot, so early mornings or late afternoons would be preferable if cycling during that season.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle this route. It uses public roads through the towns and countryside.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is considered 'moderate' and requires a good level of fitness. While it's often recommended as a classic introduction for cyclists new to the area, beginners might find the 37.5 km distance and 680 meters of elevation gain challenging, especially the climb to Puerto de los Pescadores.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: You can expect mostly well-paved, smooth, and quiet roads. There is an initial 'busier start' leading to the climb to Puerto de los Pescadores, but the majority of the ride offers excellent asphalt surfaces through rural landscapes and charming village roads.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse and picturesque scenery. You'll pass through the village roads of Monda, the rural landscapes of Guaro, and the rolling countryside around Coín. Alhaurín el Grande, your starting point, also offers panoramic views of the surrounding mountain range.

faqs.question: What towns or highlights will I pass through on the route?
faqs.answer: The route is named for the towns it connects: Coín, Guaro, and Monda. A key highlight is the climb to Puerto de los Pescadores.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: On average, this 37.5 km route with 680 meters of elevation gain takes approximately 2 hours to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for refreshments along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through the towns of Coín, Guaro, and Monda, you will find opportunities for cafes, restaurants, and shops to refuel.

faqs.question: What should I know about the initial climb on this route?
faqs.answer: The route begins with an initial 'busier start' from Alhaurín el Grande that leads directly into the climb to Puerto de los Pescadores. Be prepared for this ascent early in your ride; it's a key feature and a good test of fitness.
